title: Consuming Forbidden or Stolen Wealth
subtitle: Eating What Allah Has Forbidden — Nourishing the Body with What Leads to the Fire

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِالْبَاطِلِ وَتُدْلُوا بِهَا إِلَى الْحُكَّامِ لِتَأْكُلُوا فَرِيقًا مِّنْ أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ بِالْإِثْمِ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
verses.translation: And do not consume one another's wealth unjustly or send it [in bribery] to the rulers in order that [they might aid] you [to] consume a portion of the wealth of the people in sin, while you know [it is unlawful].

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوَالَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِالْبَاطِلِ إِلَّا أَن تَكُونَ تِجَارَةً عَن تَرَاضٍ مِّنكُمْ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, do not consume one another's wealth unjustly but only [in lawful] business by mutual consent.
They are devourers of what is unlawful.

brief_explanation: The Quran repeatedly forbids consuming wealth 'bil-batil' — through falsehood, injustice, and unlawful means — as a foundational moral command for believers. This includes wealth taken by theft, fraud, bribery, deception in trade, unlawful wages, or any means that violates the rights of others. The Prophet (peace be upon him) warned that flesh nourished by haram will not enter Paradise and that its fuel is the Fire — making the consumption of haram wealth a direct path to Hell in the most literal sense. The Quran explicitly mentions those who use bribes and legal corruption to swallow others' wealth, revealing that even 'legal cover' does not cleanse what is fundamentally unlawful before Allah.
capital_punishment: There is no single hadd for consuming haram wealth in general. The penalty depends on the means: if acquired through theft (meeting the nisab threshold), the hadd of hand amputation applies. If through fraud, force, or bribery, the state applies ta'zir — which may include imprisonment, forced restitution, and public censure. All schools agree that haram wealth must be returned to its rightful owner if they are known, or given in charity if the owner cannot be identified. Keeping it is prohibited even after repentance.
